replacement_target: later explicit XStack/AIDE inventory checkpoint or replacement artifact only

current_program_context.current_product_priority: repo-local playable baseline and repo stabilization
current_program_context.series_role: narrowing, modularizing, classifying, and contract extraction preparation that must not compete with playable-baseline work

evidence_sources: post-Zeta closure and ultra audit artifacts
direct inspection of tools/xstack, appshell, validation, launcher, setup, runtime, release, security, server, and client surfaces
direct inspection of READMEs, docstrings, and entrypoint modules
repo search evidence for imports, wrappers, stubs, and generated-evidence roots

maturity_rules.implemented_and_used: real code plus direct usage evidence from imports, audit-run entrypoints, or active orchestration
maturity_rules.implemented_but_isolated: real code with limited or example-style usage
maturity_rules.partial_or_incomplete: real surface with coupling issues, TODOs, placeholder logic, or incomplete behavior
maturity_rules.stub_wrapper_or_transitional: wrapper, shim, or transitional surface even if runnable
maturity_rules.doctrinal_or_planning_only: documentation, catalog, policy, or planning only

portability_rules: portable_core requires structurally generic implementation with limited Dominium product or session assumptions
location under tools/xstack does not by itself prove portability
older portability docs do not outrank live implementation evidence
bundle IDs, pack roots, release or trust wiring, loopback assumptions, and product-shell coupling keep a surface in Dominium for now
used_vs_isolated_vs_partial_rules: used means direct imports, audit-observed entrypoints, or policy wiring
isolated means example, fixture, or non-primary command-path usage
partial means active but blocked, coupled, or placeholder-heavy
doctrinal means text or metadata rather than executing behavior
classification_method.unclear_or_mixed_definition: surface has multiple real signals and should not be flattened into a single ownership or portability claim

role_classifications.portable_core: Narrow deterministic substrate with low direct Dominium or product coupling.
role_classifications.runtime_concern: Session, boot, SRZ, stage, or authoritative process/session control surfaces.
role_classifications.ops_concern: Gate, validation, audit, CI, orchestration, and repo-operation surfaces.
role_classifications.dominium_only: Product-shell, bundle, release, loopback, or repo-control surfaces that should remain in Dominium now.
role_classifications.legacy_or_deprecate: Older sibling, derived, or transitional surfaces that remain useful evidence but are not the current semantic center.
role_classifications.unclear_or_mixed: Mixed ownership, bridge, support, or evidence surfaces that should not be flattened into a simple portability claim.

maturity_classifications.implemented_and_used: Live code with direct usage evidence.
maturity_classifications.implemented_but_isolated: Real code, but example-only or narrowly consumed.
maturity_classifications.partial_or_incomplete: Real surface with coupling issues, placeholders, or incomplete behavior.
maturity_classifications.stub_wrapper_or_transitional: Wrapper, shim, or transitional surface rather than the strongest implementation layer.
maturity_classifications.doctrinal_or_planning_only: Documentation, task, policy, or planning surface only.

extraction_classifications.plausible_future_aide_extraction_candida: Strongest later extraction candidates once baseline and uncoupling work permit it.
extraction_classifications.maybe_extractable_later: Reusable shape exists, but current coupling or wrapper status is still too high.
extraction_classifications.retain_in_dominium: Keep this surface in Dominium for the current series and baseline period.
extraction_classifications.defer_until_post_baseline: Review again later, but do not treat as a current extraction target.
extraction_classifications.explicitly_not_an_aide_target: Do not use this surface as the basis for AIDE extraction.

ledger.name: XStack core planner and runner substrate
ledger.purpose: Deterministic planning, execution ordering, impact selection, and artifact classification for XStack profile execution.
evidence_notes: scripts/dev/gate.py imports plan, scheduler, runners, profiler, and cache helpers from tools.xstack.core
docs/governance/XSTACK_INCREMENTAL_MODEL.md and docs/XSTACK.md both identify tools/xstack/core as the planner substrate

ledger.name: Low-level CompatX contract primitives
ledger.purpose: Canonical JSON hashing, schema loading, and strict instance validation for multiple XStack-adjacent consumers.
evidence_notes: direct imports appear in AppShell, release, security, server, runtime, and client surfaces
this is the broadest reused XStack-adjacent substrate in the live repo

ledger.name: CompatX profile checks and version routing
ledger.purpose: Profile-scoped contract checking and schema-version routing on top of the low-level CompatX primitives.
evidence_notes: run_compatx_check is used by ControlX and validation
versioning.py still routes migrations to migration_stub with refusal.compatx.migration_not_implemented
README says migration execution is currently a stub

ledger.name: Live XStack ControlX orchestration surface
ledger.purpose: Drive FAST, STRICT, and FULL deterministic profile execution across the live XStack families.
evidence_notes: tools/xstack/run.py is the stable XStack entrypoint
orchestrator.py imports and coordinates nearly every live XStack family
audit entrypoints classify xstack_run as canonical and runnable now

ledger.name: Repo gate and review bridge scripts that consume XStack
ledger.purpose: Bridge repo-wide gate execution, review policies, and CI invariants onto XStack planning, cache, and rule surfaces.
evidence_notes: scripts/dev/gate.py imports the XStack core planner and cache store
scripts/ci/check_repox_rules.py hardcodes many tools/xstack paths and runtime-no-xstack checks
tools/review/xi8_common.py references tools/xstack/ci/xstack_ci_entrypoint.py

ledger.name: Session create and pipeline-contract materialization
ledger.purpose: Create session specs, universe artifacts, and pinned contract-bundle artifacts for the repo-local playable path.
evidence_notes: the audit ran python tools/xstack/session_create.py successfully
ULTRA_REPO_AUDIT_ENTRYPOINTS_AND_RUNPATHS.md calls it canonical and part of the shortest path to a baseline session
creator.py depends directly on Dominium pack, worldgen, universe, geo, and profile surfaces

ledger.name: Session boot, SRZ, runtime, and control machinery
ledger.purpose: Boot session specs, drive control flows, run SRZ execution, and coordinate session stage transitions.
evidence_notes: audit marks this family as partial and wired_but_fragile
session_boot.py is only reliably recommended with canonical saves/<save_id> layout
process_runtime.py directly spans many authoritative runtime and domain modules

ledger.name: UI bind and descriptor-to-session host boundary
ledger.purpose: Validate UI window descriptors and bind them to session-host expectations and entitlements.
evidence_notes: audit baselines repeatedly record passing ui_bind checks
the validator remains coupled to session-host semantics rather than being a purely generic UI contract layer

ledger.name: TestX deterministic harness and suite corpus
ledger.purpose: Run profile-aware deterministic test subsets and larger sweeps over XStack and adjacent runtime or control surfaces.
evidence_notes: audit classifies testx_all.py as canonical and present
ULTRA_REPO_AUDIT_SYSTEM_INVENTORY.md groups validation, TestX, CTest, and playtest suites as implemented_and_used
testx_all.py is a convenience wrapper on top of testx/runner.py

ledger.name: XStack AuditX wrapper
ledger.purpose: Run semantic and architecture drift scanning through the XStack profile runner.
evidence_notes: check.py describes itself as an XStack wrapper for the full AuditX pipeline
the owning analyzer stack still lives outside tools/xstack

ledger.name: XStack RepoX wrapper
ledger.purpose: Run deterministic policy and structure checks under XStack profiles.
evidence_notes: docstring labels it a minimal deterministic RepoX policy scan
scan roots and rules are heavily repo-specific

ledger.name: SecureX minimal signature-status checks
ledger.purpose: Verify pack manifest signature-status metadata during XStack runs.
evidence_notes: the code performs real pack iteration and refusal or warn handling
README explicitly brands it as a minimal signature-status verifier

ledger.name: PerformX placeholder budget and fidelity checks
ledger.purpose: Perform policy- and registry-based budget or fidelity smoke checks during profile runs.
evidence_notes: module docstring says placeholder checks
current logic is real but intentionally narrow and depends on build outputs

ledger.name: Xi-7 and Xi-8 XStack CI guardrail surface
ledger.purpose: Define CI guardrail entrypoints, rule catalogs, profiles, and support documentation for deterministic repo checks.
evidence_notes: xstack_ci_entrypoint.py is a real runner
data/xstack/gate_definitions.json records required rules, profiles, and provisional allowances
docs and tests repeatedly reference the entrypoint

ledger.name: Pack loading, contribution parsing, and registry compile pipeline
ledger.purpose: Discover packs, parse contributions, select bundles, compile registries, and validate lockfile or registry outputs.
evidence_notes: ControlX orchestrator uses this family directly
registry_compile/constants.py bakes in DEFAULT_BUNDLE_ID bundle.base.lab and a large Dominium registry vocabulary

ledger.name: PackagingX dist layout and packaging verification
ledger.purpose: Build and validate deterministic dist layouts for launcher, setup, and control-plane flows.
evidence_notes: dist_build.py imports session creation, session boot, intent-script execution, and pack or registry compile helpers
tools/setup/build.py imports PackagingX directly

ledger.name: Unified validation engine plus legacy adapters
ledger.purpose: Provide a repo-wide validation entrypoint that aggregates XStack compat checks with broader legacy and product validation surfaces.
evidence_notes: the engine imports tools.xstack.compatx helpers and maps many legacy surfaces
audit classifies the validation stack as implemented_and_used
the live FAST entrypoint exists but current full execution remains environment-sensitive

ledger.name: Python and AppShell launcher and setup command surfaces
ledger.purpose: Expose the strongest live repo-local shells for packs, profiles, compat status, install or update flows, trust operations, and local product supervision.
evidence_notes: audit identifies tools/launcher/launch.py and tools/setup/setup_cli.py as canonical repo-local shells
both successfully ran compat-status, profiles list, and packs list

ledger.name: Compiled launcher and setup wrappers
ledger.purpose: Native shells around stronger Python and AppShell flows.
evidence_notes: audit marks both compiled shells as partial and wired_but_fragile
smoke output still reports stub status or limited control hooks
multiple generated UI files are explicitly labeled as stubs

ledger.name: AppShell bootstrap, command, IPC, and supervisor layer
ledger.purpose: Shared repo-local command shell, compatibility negotiation, IPC, supervisor, TUI, and mode dispatch infrastructure used by launcher and setup.
evidence_notes: audit classifies AppShell as implemented_and_used
bootstrap.py and product_bootstrap.py are real shared infrastructure
rendered_stub.py, tui_stub.py, and fallback paths prove that parts of AppShell intentionally preserve stub behavior

ledger.name: Local singleplayer and authoritative loopback bridge surfaces
ledger.purpose: Materialize the local authoritative loopback flow that the playable baseline relies on before external transport exists.
evidence_notes: audit marks server boot and loopback transport as real implementation
reuse plan says the local controller, loopback transport, and process spawn surfaces should be reused immediately
direct controller invocation produced real handshake and compat payloads, but the public wrapper path remains unclear

ledger.name: Release and offline trust surfaces that consume XStack contract helpers
ledger.purpose: Release manifesting, update resolution, archive policy, and trust verification for repo-local release or control-plane flows.
evidence_notes: audit classifies release and security as implemented_and_used
these modules import XStack canonical JSON helpers directly, but their role remains release or trust control-plane work inside Dominium

ledger.name: Legacy standalone ControlX root
ledger.purpose: Older autonomous prompt and control-plane wrapper with sanitization, queue execution, and run logs.
evidence_notes: code is real and release docs still reference it
X-0 froze it as a sibling or transitional surface rather than the current branded XStack center

ledger.name: Legacy standalone CompatX root
ledger.purpose: Older compatibility CLI and semantic-contract tooling retained as an adapter bridge.
evidence_notes: validation/validation_engine.py still maps tools/compatx/compatx.py as legacy.compatx_cli
audit and docs show tools/compatx/core/semantic_contract_validator.py remains an active bridge helper

ledger.name: Current XStack task and policy fact-base docs
ledger.purpose: Provide current governance/task taxonomy and X-0 scope boundaries for later prompts.
evidence_notes: these artifacts are binding or task-facing fact surfaces, not implementation
later prompts should consume them, not infer portability from them

ledger.name: Older XStack governance and portability reference docs
ledger.purpose: Historical and derived reference surfaces describing portability, incremental execution, extension hooks, and production criteria.
major_dependencies: older governance mirrors and derived docs
evidence_notes: several files explicitly say they are only partially aligned
X-0 already recorded that these docs overstate removability or portability relative to live code

ledger.name: Support examples, skills, fixtures, cache split, and generated evidence roots
ledger.purpose: Carry example extension hooks, operator skill notes, deterministic fixtures, registry-compile cache helpers, and generated run evidence.
evidence_notes: extensions/example_x/extension.py is a no-op example runner
skills/*.md are instruction surfaces
testdata/** is actively used by TestX and docs
tools/xstack/cache_store/** is a real registry-compile cache distinct from tools/xstack/core/cache_store.py
tools/xstack/out/** is generated evidence only

reusable_now.why: Strongest deterministic planner, scheduler, and artifact substrate in the live repo.
Broadest reused contract and canonicalization substrate across XStack-adjacent consumers.
Live orchestration contract for FAST, STRICT, and FULL profile execution.
Strong deterministic tool-harness family already used for validation sweeps.
Clean current fact source for CI guardrail profiles and rule metadata.

baseline_relevant.why: Shortest current path to baseline session artifacts.
Relevant to baseline boot and control, but still coupled to canonical saves/<save_id> layout.
Audit identifies these as the strongest canonical repo-local shells.
Shared shell, supervisor, and compatibility substrate beneath launcher and setup.
Strongest evidence-backed local authority bridge before external transport exists.
Use to gate baseline assembly after boot and loopback smoke stabilize.
Use as repo validation infrastructure after core boot or attach path is stable.
Convergence with tools/xstack/controlx would distract from the playable baseline.
Older portability docs should not drive current baseline decisions.
Fixtures, examples, cache helpers, and generated evidence are not current baseline owners.

prohibited_misreadings: treating every tools/xstack/** surface as portable core
extracting session or runtime control just because it lives under tools/xstack
promoting wrappers and stubs into core because they have stable command names
treating launcher, setup, or AppShell shells as current AIDE candidates
using this inventory to justify immediate broad refactors, moves, or renames
reading tools/xstack/out/** as an ownership surface
reading release or trust consumers as proof that XStack already owns the release control plane
using future AIDE ambitions to compete with canonical playable-baseline work

downstream_prompts_enabled.purpose: portable contract extraction mapping using the frozen scope and classified inventory ledger
Codex or repo-operating contract formalization over the classified XStack subset
post-baseline review of whether any current candidates are mature enough for actual extraction
